MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Against Plaintiff Norma Cristina Verduzco
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT WISE CHOICE TRANS CORP., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Against Plaintiff Norma Cristina Verduzco.

Appearances:
Drew Lewis, Esq., Drew Lewis, Pc, via Zoom videoconference, (833) 600-7400, for Plaintiff.
Sumy Kim, Esq., of O'Hagan Meyer LLP, (415) 578-6900, for Defendants.

The matter is ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the court confirms its tentative ruling. Defendant Wise Choice Trans Corp., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication in its favor of plaintiff Norma Verduzco's eleventh through fourteenth causes of action is granted and its motion for summary adjudication as to Ms. Verduzco's request for punitive damages as to those claims is off calendar as moot. Wise Choice satisfied its initial summary motion burden as to the eleventh through fourteenth causes of action by presenting admissible evidence that it terminated Ms. Verduzco's employment based on its honest belief that Ms. Verduzco was working for a competitive company while she was on disability leave. Ms. Verduzco failed to present any evidence that the reason given for the termination of her employment was pretextual or that she was terminated in whole or in part due to her claimed disability, to retaliate against her for taking a medical leave or for any other impermissible discriminatory reason or any reason that violates California public policy. The adjudication of the eleventh through fourteenth causes against Ms. Verduzco moots the portion of this motion seeking summary adjudication of Ms. Verduzco's request for punitive damages on those claims. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, in compliance with CRC 3.1312, and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.
